mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut months are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.5%, it signals labor market deterioration that historically precedes corporate earnings downgrades, depressing equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in >50% of months where UNRATE exceeds 6.5% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in both the federal funds rate and core inflation compress real yields and signal aggressive tightening, which negatively impacts equity multiples over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in >60% of co-occurring rate hike and inflation acceleration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, a sustained decline in housing starts acts as a leading indicator of broader demand contraction, forecasting negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines do not lead to negative 5-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or the predictive power vanishes in post-2020 data.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding capacity utilization and corporate throughput, which historically correlates with positive equity momentum over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Monthly INDPRO growth shows no positive correlation with 5-day SPX returns across any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real estate construction spending falls below its 12-month moving average, it signals a credit-driven demand shock that suppresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative following RSAFS declines exceeding 2% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in payroll employment and real estate spending indicates robust aggregate demand, which drives positive equity returns over a one-quarter horizon as earnings growth accelerates.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and RSAFS uptrend fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in >50% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid GDP growth, a subsequent rise in the federal funds rate acts as a benign normalization signal that sustains positive equity momentum over the next month, rather than triggering a downturn.
failure_condition: Rate hikes during high-GDP periods consistently lead to negative 20-day forward returns, contradicting the benign normalization premise.
